abstract: Promotes code between environments (dev → staging → main) by opening a PR from the correct source branch. Enforces the Gitflow promotion chain — staging only accepts PRs from dev, main only from staging. Resolves branch names (dev/development, staging/stg, main/master) from an optional deploy block in .asome/config.json, falling back to auto-detection via git remote branches. Never direct-pushes; always PR-first. Watches PR checks (`gh pr checks --watch`) before declaring a promotion ready to merge, and watches the post-merge workflow run (`gh run watch`) before tagging — any check or run failure halts the task. After a staging → main promotion merges and post-merge checks pass, tags a semver release (asks the user for major/minor/patch) and publishes a GitHub Release with auto-generated notes via `gh release create --generate-notes` — no separate changelog tooling.
